title: hieracium subcrassiforme
intro: Hieracium subcrassiforme, also known as mouse-ear hawk's-beard, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic and anti-inflammatory agent, due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Hieracium subcrassiforme include its ability to treat urinary tract issues, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and other respiratory problems. It can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used internally or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
